entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Cajanus cajan (L.) Millsp., a legume crop for which the first hybrid was released in India

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the first pigeonpea hybrid was developed and released

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC MALE STERILITY
entities.description: Male sterility determined by the cytoplasm, used to circumvent labor intensiveness in seed production

entities.name: WIDE HYBRIDIZATION
entities.description: A breeding method involving the crossing of different species to develop male sterility

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL BACKCROSSING
entities.description: A breeding technique used within wide hybridization to transfer traits to a recurrent parent

entities.name: CAJANUS SERICEUS
entities.description: A plant species used in wide hybridization with Cajanus cajan

entities.name: MULTIPLE CROSS GENOME TRANSFER
entities.description: A breeding method used to achieve stable cytoplasmic-genic male sterility

entities.name: REVERSION TO FERTILITY
entities.description: The process where male-sterile lines regain fertility, influenced by environmental or genetic factors

entities.name: LOW TEMPERATURE
entities.description: An environmental factor that can influence the reversion to fertility

entities.name: HIGH HUMIDITY
entities.description: An environmental factor that can influence the reversion to fertility

entities.name: GENETIC FACTORS
entities.description: Biological factors that can determine the reversion to fertility in male-sterile lines

entities.name: VICIA FABA
entities.description: A plant species used for comparison regarding the effect of temperature on fertility restoration

entities.name: CAJANUS
entities.description: The genus encompassing pigeonpea and Cajanus sericeus

entities.name: ICPL 85030
entities.description: A pigeonpea genotype used to maintain stable cytoplasmic-genic male sterility

entities.name: ICPL 90035
entities.description: A pigeonpea genotype used to maintain stable cytoplasmic-genic male sterility

entities.name: MALE STERILE GENES
entities.description: Genes that cause male sterility, facilitating the development of hybrid pigeonpea
